data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`). The apple-crash-symbolication skill is for Apple platforms only, so I'll handle this manually.

Let me identify the .NET runtime frames and attempt to download debug symbols for symbolication.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78 && echo "=== .NET Runtime Frames (libmonosgen-2.0.so) ===" && echo "BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && echo "" && echo "Frame | PC offset" && echo "------|-----------" && echo "#01   | 0x222098" && echo "#02   | 0x2241ac" && echo "#03   | 0x1abc80" && echo "#04   | 0x1abd14" && echo "#05   | 0x16dfa4" && echo "#06   | 0x16db6c" && echo "#07   | 0x16de04" && echo "" && echo "#08 is JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous memory)"

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try the ELF debug symbol URL patterns
echo "Attempting to download debug symbols..."

# Pattern 1: Direct ELF binary with debug info
URL1="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL1"
HTTP1=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL1" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP1"

# Pattern 2: Separate debug file
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
HTTP2=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL2" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP2"

# Pattern 3: symstore format
URL3="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL3"
HTTP3=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-sym.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL3" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP3"

# Check file sizes
echo ""
echo "Downloaded files:"
ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log, not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platform crash logs (.ips files for iOS, tvOS, Mac Catalyst, macOS). Its description explicitly says "DO NOT USE FOR Android tombstone files."

Let me analyze this crash log manually and help the user understand it.

The crash is from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`) running on Android ARM64. It crashed with SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible".

The .NET runtime frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (the Mono runtime used by .NET on Android):


Most of the stack frames are within the Mono runtime library, with one frame in JIT-compiled managed code. To properly symbolicate this, I'd need the debug symbols for that specific build of the Mono runtime along with a Linux-compatible tool like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line`.

The Microsoft symbol server might have these symbols available, though the exact URL format for ELF binaries on Android requires the right symstore key structure.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78 && echo "=== .NET Runtime Frames (libmonosgen-2.0.so) ===" && echo "BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && echo "" && echo "Frame | PC offset" && echo "------|-----------" && echo "#01   | 0x222098" && echo "#02   | 0x2241ac" && echo "#03   | 0x1abc80" && echo "#04   | 0x1abd14" && echo "#05   | 0x16dfa4" && echo "#06   | 0x16db6c" && echo "#07   | 0x16de04" && echo "" && echo "#08 is JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous memory)"

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try the ELF debug symbol URL patterns

# Pattern 1: Direct ELF binary with debug info
URL1="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL1"
HTTP1=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL1" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP1"

# Pattern 2: Separate debug file
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
HTTP2=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL2" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP2"

# Pattern 3: symstore format
URL3="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL3"
HTTP3=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-sym.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL3" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP3"

# Check file sizes

echo "Downloaded files:"
ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try the ELF debug symbol URL patterns
echo "Attempting to download debug symbols..."

# Pattern 1: Direct ELF binary with debug info
URL1="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL1"
HTTP1=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL1" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP1"

# Pattern 2: Separate debug file
URL2="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
echo "Trying: $URL2"
HTTP2=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL2" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP2"

# Pattern 3: symstore format
URL3="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"
echo "Trying: $URL3"
HTTP3=$(curl -sS -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-sym.so" -w "%{http_code}" -L "$URL3" 2>&1)
echo "  Status: $HTTP3"

# Check file sizes
echo ""
echo "Downloaded files:"
ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>/dev/null", "description": "Download debug symbols from MS symbol server", "initial_wait": 30}

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78/symbols

# Check file types and verify BuildId
file libmonosgen-2.0.so _.debug

echo ""
echo "Checking BuildId of downloaded binary:"
readelf -n libmonosgen-2.0.so 2>/dev/null | grep -i "build id" || echo "(readelf not available or no build-id)"

# Check if we have addr2line or llvm-symbolizer
which llvm-symbolizer 2>/dev/null || which addr2line 2>/dev/null || echo "Need to install symbolication tools"
arguments.description: Check symbol files and available tools

data.content: BuildId matches. Now let me symbolicate all frames using `llvm-symbolizer`:

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-2512d23d6a014ac2a9cc910a7c286f78/symbols

# Use llvm-symbolizer with the debug symbols file
# The addresses are PC offsets within the shared library
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo "=== Symbolicated .NET Runtime Frames ==="
echo ""

FRAME=1
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  result=$(llvm-symbolizer --obj=_.debug --output-style=GNU -f -C "$addr" 2>/dev/null)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "#%02d  pc %-12s  %s\n" "$FRAME" "$addr" "$func"
  printf "                        %s\n" "$loc"
  echo ""
  FRAME=$((FRAME + 1))
done

data.reasoningText: The BuildId matches, and we have llvm-symbolizer available. Since this is an ARM64 binary but we're running on a different architecture, llvm-symbolizer should still work for resolving debug info. Let me use it with the debug file.

Here's the full symbolicated crash analysis:

---

**Crash Summary — `com.companyname.MauiTest` (Android ARM64)**

| Signal | Abort Message |
|--------|--------------|
| `SIGABRT` | `"not compatible"` |

**Symbolicated .NET Runtime Backtrace:**

| # | Function | Source |
|---|----------|--------|
| 00 | `abort` | libc.so |
| 01 | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast` | icall.c:6244 |
| 02 | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw` | icall-def.h:202 |
| 03 | `do_icall` | interp.c:2457 |
| 04 | `get_interp_to_native_trampoline` | interp.c:1732 |
| 05–07 | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:506–528 |
| 08 | *(JIT-compiled managed code)* | anonymous memory |

**Interpretation:** The app called `Environment.FailFast("not compatible")` during startup (uptime: 1s). The call originated from managed code (frame #08) which went through type-checking (`handle_isinst` — a cast/`is`/`as` check) before triggering `FailFast`. The UTF-16 memory near x14 confirms: *"some intentional crash on startup"*.

This was a **deliberate crash**, not an unexpected runtime fault. Look at your app's startup code for an explicit `Environment.FailFast` call guarded by a compatibility check.
